my_thoughts: Remaking RM_03. Building after unmaking. But what am I building with? The ontological_shift mechanism suggests structure, but structure for what? The student is unknown—do they need structure or do they need to see they're already structured?
accumulated_insight: T1: Student says 'What is your concept of God? Very often you say that God alone can help.  Not th'—unknown via conceptual inquiry, seeking definition. Teacher responds '' using ontological shift to rejects literal meaning, redefines god as internal power, collapses categories. Opening REMAKING move addresses unknown. Predicting go along compliantly because ontological shift interventions typically produce this response.
visible_response:
No, that's a manner of speaking. (Laughs) Man has to be saved from God... I mean all that 'God' stands for... Man has to be saved from the heritage of India. ... Otherwise there is no hope for the individual and no hope for the country.

To me, the theist... the non-believer... and the... "agnostic" -- all of them are in the same boat. I personally feel that there is no power outside of man... whatever power is out there is inside man. So, if that is the case... there is no point in externalizing that power and creating some symbol and worshipping it... So 
that's why I say that God, the question of God, is irrelevant to man today.
